notes: Category-keyed alternatives / drop-in map. AUDIT REQUIRED — these are starting recommendations. Wingman uses this to suggest substitutes when the primary pick is flagged by forge_manufacturer_status or pie_flags. 'drop_in' means pin-and-firmware-compatible swap; 'equivalent' means same class with minor config changes. Audited 2026-04-13: FCC Covered List Dec 2025 flags added to Walksnail, TBS, DJI; NDAA statuses populated for ELRS/mesh/datalink entries; Jetson Orin allocation risk added; Brain FPV status corrected.

companion_computers.description: Linux-capable companion computers for autonomy, AI inference, SLAM, and mission payloads on ArduPilot/PX4 drones.

rpi-cm4.name: Raspberry Pi Compute Module 4
rpi-cm4.notes: CM4 supply has normalized since 2022-2023 crunch. Still worth stocking extras for long-duration programs. NDAA note: UK-designed, global manufacture — verify production site for defense procurement. FCC Covered List diversion risk: 40,000+ RPi 4B units confirmed in Geran-2/3 Shahed-136 drones — raises export control scrutiny on bulk orders.

rpi-cm5.name: Raspberry Pi Compute Module 5
rpi-cm5.notes: Successor to CM4. Better availability than CM4 was, but still subject to allocation.

rpi-5.name: Raspberry Pi 5
rpi-5.notes: Full-size SBC. Strong ecosystem, best Debian/Ubuntu support.

radxa-cm3.name: Radxa CM3
radxa-cm3.notes: Pin-compatible drop-in for CM4. RK3566 SoC. First choice when CM4 is unavailable — no carrier board change required.

radxa-cm5.name: Radxa CM5
radxa-cm5.notes: RK3588S SoC. Significantly more compute than CM3.

radxa-rock-5b.name: Radxa Rock 5B
radxa-rock-5b.notes: RK3588 SoC. 8-core, 6 TOPS NPU, great for YOLO on-drone inference. Preferred RPi-5 alternative when availability is tight.

pine64-soquartz.name: Pine64 SOQuartz
pine64-soquartz.notes: RK3566 SoC. Less active ecosystem than Radxa, but works on standard CM4 carriers.

jetson-nano.name: NVIDIA Jetson Nano (original)
jetson-nano.notes: EOL — NVIDIA discontinued. Do not spec for new builds.

jetson-orin-nano.name: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano
jetson-orin-nano.notes: Replaced original Nano. 40 TOPS NPU. Best AI/CV drone companion under ~10W.

jetson-orin-nx.name: NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX
jetson-orin-nx.notes: Higher performance tier, ~15-25W. For heavy multi-camera AI stacks.

modalai-voxl2.name: ModalAI VOXL 2
modalai-voxl2.notes: Snapdragon-based. Integrated FC + companion computer in one PCB. Blue UAS Framework listed.

7inch_long_range.reasoning: 6 TOPS NPU for optional on-drone vision, 8GB RAM, available inventory while RPi 5 is still allocation-constrained, ~5W idle fits 7" power budget.

5inch_freestyle_no_ai.reasoning: Minimum viable Linux for MAVLink/logging is fine. Use whichever is in stock.

ai_inference_builds.reasoning: Only real option if you need YOLO / SLAM at >10fps. Expect 7-10W power draw.

blue_uas_defense.reasoning: Blue UAS Framework listed. Combined FC + companion in one unit reduces BOM complexity.

hd_vtx_air_units.description: Digital HD FPV video transmitters (air units) for goggles-based piloting.

dji-o3-air-unit.name: DJI O3 Air Unit
dji-o3-air-unit.notes: Highest quality but vendor is covered foreign entity. FCC Covered List prohibits authorization for US use as of Dec 22 2025. Do not spec for US defense/commercial builds. Blue UAS exemption to FCC Covered List expires Jan 1 2027.

dji-o4-air-unit.name: DJI O4 Air Unit
dji-o4-air-unit.notes: Same restrictions as O3. Blue UAS exemption to FCC Covered List expires Jan 1 2027.

walksnail-avatar-hd.name: Walksnail Avatar HD Pro Kit
walksnail-avatar-hd.notes: Chinese manufacture. Good O3 alternative for commercial builds where NDAA isn't required. Lower latency than HDZero, higher than DJI. FCC Covered List Dec 22 2025: Walksnail produced by Shenzhen manufacturer — covered as foreign UAS critical component. No new FCC authorizations. Ineligible for federal/grant-funded builds.

walksnail-moonlight.name: Walksnail Moonlight
walksnail-moonlight.notes: Low-light capable version of Avatar. FCC Covered List Dec 22 2025: Walksnail produced by Shenzhen manufacturer — covered as foreign UAS critical component. No new FCC authorizations. Ineligible for federal/grant-funded builds.

hdzero-race-v3.name: HDZero Race V3 VTX
hdzero-race-v3.notes: Open-source firmware, US/China operation. The closest to NDAA-acceptable HD digital FPV in the civilian market, but verify country-of-origin per SKU.

orqa-fpv-stack.name: ORQA FC2020 + IRONGhost
orqa-fpv-stack.notes: Only purpose-built NDAA-compliant FPV datalink in market. Not HD — analog with integrated control.

civilian_commercial_hd.reasoning: Best HD quality outside DJI; acceptable when NDAA is not a requirement.

defense_fpv.reasoning: Only NDAA-compliant FPV stack. Accept analog-class image quality for compliance.

motors_7inch.description: Brushless motors sized for 7" props on long-range / cinelifter / defense FPV builds. Typical spec: 2806.5-2807 stator, 1300-1800 KV, 6S.

tmotor-f90.name: T-Motor F90
tmotor-f90.notes: Classic 7" motor. Premium build quality. Heavier than modern options.

emax-eco-ii-2807.name: EMAX ECO II 2807
emax-eco-ii-2807.notes: Budget 7" option. Accept reduced efficiency for cost savings.

7inch_long_range.reasoning: Both are widely stocked and spec'd for 6S 7" long-range. Choose based on price/availability at purchase time.

flight_controllers_7inch.description: Flight controllers appropriate for 7" long-range / cinelifter builds.

matek-h743.name: Matek H743 (Slim / Mini / Wing variants)
matek-h743.notes: Preferred for long-range iNav / ArduPilot. H7 chip, lots of UARTs, reliable.

speedybee-f405-v4.name: SpeedyBee F405 V4
speedybee-f405-v4.notes: Budget Betaflight-first FC. Bluetooth config helpful for field tuning.

ark-fc.name: ARK Electronics Blue UAS FC
ark-fc.notes: US-made, Blue UAS Framework listed. Spec for defense builds.

cubepilot-cube-orange-plus.name: CubePilot Cube Orange+
cubepilot-cube-orange-plus.notes: Industrial autopilot. TAA-eligible (Australia). Heavy but feature-rich.

7inch_long_range_inav.reasoning: Standard pick for iNav long-range. Large UART count supports GPS + datalink + OSD.

7inch_betaflight_freestyle.reasoning: Either works. Kakute H7 if you want headroom; SpeedyBee if budget matters.

blue_uas_7inch.reasoning: Only way to keep a 7" build Blue UAS Framework listed at FC level.

rc_long_range_link.description: RC control links for long-range and defense builds (5-50 km class).

expresslrs-2-4ghz.name: ExpressLRS 2.4GHz (ELRS)
expresslrs-2-4ghz.notes: Open source. Dominant hobby long-range pick. Multiple hardware vendors (HappyModel, BetaFPV, Matek, Radiomaster). No single point of failure. NDAA compliance is hardware-vendor dependent — US-assembled modules (BetaFPV US, Radiomaster) differ from China-only SKUs. Verify per SKU for defense builds.

expresslrs-915mhz.name: ExpressLRS 915MHz (ELRS)
expresslrs-915mhz.notes: Longer range variant for the Americas. Same ecosystem. NDAA compliance is hardware-vendor dependent — US-assembled modules (BetaFPV US, Radiomaster) differ from China-only SKUs. Verify per SKU for defense builds.

tbs-crossfire.name: TBS Crossfire
tbs-crossfire.notes: 915MHz. Pioneered long-range FPV. Proprietary but mature. Nano receiver is the standard 7" pick. TBS (Team BlackSheep) modules produced in China — covered under FCC Covered List Dec 2025 as foreign UAS critical components. For NDAA builds use ELRS from US-assembled SKU or ORQA IRONGhost.

tbs-tracer.name: TBS Tracer
tbs-tracer.notes: 2.4GHz lower-latency variant of Crossfire. TBS (Team BlackSheep) modules produced in China — covered under FCC Covered List Dec 2025 as foreign UAS critical components. For NDAA builds use ELRS from US-assembled SKU or ORQA IRONGhost.

ghost-rc.name: Ghost RC (ORQA, formerly ImmersionRC)
ghost-rc.notes: 2.4GHz low-latency link. Originally ImmersionRC; ORQA acquired IRC circa 2023 and now owns the Ghost tech. ORQA's IRONGhost NDAA datalink is built on this protocol. IRONGhost (ORQA) is NDAA-compliant. Croatian/EU manufactured. Blue UAS listed.

7inch_long_range.reasoning: ELRS 915MHz has surpassed Crossfire in raw range and has open firmware. Crossfire remains a viable mature option if you're already in the TBS ecosystem.

esc_4in1_60a.description: 4-in-1 ESCs sized for 7" 6S builds. Typical spec: 45-65A continuous, BLHeli_32 or AM32 firmware.

holybro-tekko32-f4-65a.name: Holybro Tekko32 F4 65A
holybro-tekko32-f4-65a.notes: Integrated F4 option if you want a stack.

7inch_6s.reasoning: 55A is adequate headroom for 7" 6S. Either works; match to motor vendor for simpler support.

gps_modules.description: GNSS modules for drones. For long-range and autonomous modes, multi-constellation L1+L5 is preferred.

matek-m10q-5883.name: Matek M10Q-5883
matek-m10q-5883.notes: Modern u-blox M10. Multi-constellation (GPS + GLONASS + Galileo + BeiDou). Integrated compass. Current default pick.

matek-m9n-5883.name: Matek M9N-5883
matek-m9n-5883.notes: Older M9 chip. Still solid for simple GPS hold.

holybro-h-rtk-f9p.name: Holybro H-RTK F9P
holybro-h-rtk-f9p.notes: Centimeter-level RTK. Spec for survey / precision agriculture.

7inch_gps_hold.reasoning: Multi-constellation with integrated compass. Handles GPS-hold and RTH reliably on 7" builds.

blue_uas_7inch.reasoning: Only way to keep GPS in the Blue UAS Framework tier.

datalinks_mesh_defense.description: Mesh / MANET radios for defense-grade BVLOS / multi-drone coordination.

doodle-labs-helix.name: Doodle Labs Helix Mesh Rider
doodle-labs-helix.notes: Blue UAS Framework. AUVSI Green UAS certified. First pick for NDAA defense datalink.

silvus-streamcaster-lite.name: Silvus StreamCaster LITE 4200
silvus-streamcaster-lite.notes: AUVSI Green UAS certified. Higher-end MANET with mesh routing.

rajant-breadcrumb.name: Rajant Breadcrumb
rajant-breadcrumb.notes: True mesh (multi-hop). Peregrine series for UAS.

ndaa_defense.reasoning: Doodle Labs is the clearest Blue UAS Framework path; Silvus is the alt for higher-end MANET.

fpv_cameras_analog.description: Analog FPV cameras for NDAA-compliant FPV builds (since non-DJI analog stacks remain available).

runcam-phoenix-2.name: RunCam Phoenix 2
runcam-phoenix-2.notes: Joshua Bardwell edition remains a long-running analog favorite.

caddx-ratel-2.name: Caddx Ratel 2
caddx-ratel-2.notes: Micro analog cam, common freestyle default.

7inch_analog.reasoning: Both are mature micro analog cameras. Pick based on color preference.
